steps.title: Find the angle at C in triangle ABC
steps.content:
In triangle ABC, the angle at A is 20° and the angle at B is $x° = 100°$. Using the triangle angle sum:
$$20° + 100° + \angle ACB = 180°$$
$$\angle ACB = 60°$$

steps.title: Use vertically opposite angles at the intersection point
steps.content:
Lines AD and BE intersect at C, forming two pairs of vertically opposite angles. Since angles ACB and DCE are opposite each other:
$$\angle DCE = \angle ACB = 60°$$

steps.title: Find y using the angle sum in triangle CDE
steps.content:
In triangle CDE, we now know the angle at C is 60° and the angle at E is 40°. Using the triangle angle sum:
$$60° + 40° + y = 180°$$
$$y = 80°$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
